title: Realm Disposition
description: One terse gazetteer line on how a realm faces its neighbors — for rollable realm entries.
entries: Insular; strangers are counted at the gates, watched politely, and counted out again.
Expansionist in everything but declaration; its surveyors travel armed and its maps are ambitious.
A weathervane ally — reliably on the winning side, punctually, with documents proving it was there all along.
Neutral by ancient charter, and rich on it; both sides of every war bank, trade, and negotiate here.
Belligerent toward one particular neighbor and gracious to all others; the feud predates both dynasties.
A patient hegemon; it prefers buying neighbors to beating them, and its loans arrive with advisors attached.
Prickly about honor, quick to embassy, slow to war; its protests are famous for length.
Openly friendly, quietly fortified; visitors praise the hospitality and never see the second wall.
A league-builder, forever proposing councils, pacts, and joint tariffs; some even hold.
Isolationist since a war it does not discuss; the border posts are courteous and absolutely final.
Client to a greater power and restless in the collar; its envoys say 'partnership' with a straight face.
Missionary; it exports its faith with its goods and counts converts as diplomacy.
Predatory toward the weak, punctilious toward the strong; small neighbors keep their treaties short and their walls tall.
Everyone's second-best friend; it cultivates being too useful to conquer.
Revanchist; the lost provinces appear on its coins, its maps, and its toasts.
Scrupulously lawful abroad — it honors every treaty to the letter, and its lawyers write the letters.
Marriage-diplomacy raised to statecraft; half the neighboring thrones are cousins and the other half in negotiation.
A quiet broker; wars end in its border towns, over its wine, at its suggested terms.
Suspicious of everyone and right about some; its spy service is the realm's best-funded institution.
Cheerfully mercenary; its policy follows its exports, and its ambassadors double as trade factors.
Protector of small realms, at a modest annual fee; the protection is real, and so is the invoice.
Bellicose in speech, cautious in deed; it has mobilized four times in a generation and marched once.
An old empire in dignified retreat, trading provinces for peace and calling it magnanimity.
Young, loud, and testing every fence; the old powers compare notes about it with a certain nostalgia.
